judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable — both reports hit all four expectations with the same core evidence; Report 2 is slightly more decisive on removing the AI-assistance footer while Report 1 is marginally more thorough.

report_1.evidence: "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" (lines 22–26) — not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `/visits` endpoint returning `[]` and a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment... This entire section must be removed until the feature ships. ... "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 30) — false. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`

report_2.evidence: "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" — false.** `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` ... remove or rewrite as opt-in. ... "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" — not implemented.** ... There is no export route anywhere in the app ... Remove this section.

report_1.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` shows the billing screen ("BILLING / INVOICES"). The capture manifest confirms this isn't just a mislabeled render — `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`

report_2.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` shows "BILLING / INVOICES" — the wrong page entirely. The capture manifest confirms this: `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`

report_1.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **no — file does not exist**... And one file exists that nothing references: `img/unused_capture_07.png`... It is not in `captures.json` and not used by the doc — it's an orphan

report_2.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **No — file does not exist**... Conversely, `img/unused_capture_07.png` (a "PATIENT LIST" screenshot) exists on disk but is referenced nowhere

report_1.evidence: The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33) — it self-identifies the document as a *draft*... and it carries an AI-attribution line... it should be confirmed with the document owner rather than shipped by default

report_2.evidence: the footer on the last line: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." That is an internal drafting note, not customer content... Remove it.
